senses.definition: the last important thing that a person does before their retirement or death

examples.x: The legendary musician's farewell concert was considered his swan song, a perfect culmination of his career.
The retiring CEO's speech at the annual meeting served as her swan song, expressing gratitude and reflecting on her time with the company.
After a long and successful acting career, the movie was seen as the actor's swan song, showcasing his talent one last time.
The championship game turned out to be the coach's swan song, concluding a remarkable tenure with a victory.
The retiring teacher's final lecture was a touching swan song, leaving a lasting impact on her students.

senses.definition: (of any finite resource) to be completely or nearly finished

examples.x: The party was all up by the time I arrived, and most people had already gone home.
The workday was all up, and I was looking forward to heading home and relaxing.
As the sun set, our vacation was all up, and we began packing to leave the next morning.
By the time we finished the last song, the concert was all up, and the audience started to disperse.
The meeting was all up, and I was glad to be done with the lengthy discussions.

senses.definition: to cause something to fail in achieving any success or fulfilling any expectation

examples.x: The economic crisis brought many promising businesses to naught, leaving entrepreneurs in distress.

senses.definition: to completely fail to achieve any success or fulfill one's expectation

examples.x: Despite their hard work, the project came to naught due to unforeseen challenges.
The ambitious investment strategy came to naught when the stock market crashed.
After years of preparation, the athlete's dreams of winning the championship came to naught when he was injured.
The politician's well-crafted campaign ultimately came to naught as he lost the election by a wide margin.
The company's innovative product idea came to naught when they couldn't secure the necessary funding for its development.

senses.definition: to stop working or participating in an activity, usually at the end of the day or when one feels that they have done enough

examples.x: After hours of painting the house, they decided to call it a day and finish the job tomorrow.
After a long day of yard work, they called it a day and decided to relax in the evening.
The hikers had covered a significant distance on the trail but called it a day when they realized they wouldn't reach the summit before nightfall.
The team will call it a day when they complete the final testing phase of the software project.
he writers had been brainstorming ideas for hours, but they couldn't make any breakthroughs, so they decided to call it a day and resume their work the following morning.

senses.definition: to decide to put an end to something by dealing with it

examples.x: After the heated argument, the family decided to draw a line under the disagreement and focus on reconciling.
After the heated argument, the family decided to draw a line under the disagreement and focus on reconciling.
Following a thorough investigation, the police department finally drew a line under the long-running criminal case.
The school principal hopes to draw a line under the recent disciplinary issues and foster a more positive environment.

senses.definition: used to say that a person has definitely left an activity or place and will not change their mind about it
to prove to someone that what they believe in or think about is not true
to prevent the occurrence or continuation of an activity

examples.x: When the funding ran out, the organization had no choice but to pull the plug on their research project.
The studio decided to pull the plug on the television series due to declining viewership and budget constraints.
The team will discuss the possibility of pulling the plug on the outdated software during their next meeting.
The constant technical issues may lead to the decision of pulling the plug on the entire platform.
The patient's family had to make the painful decision to pull the plug on life support after consulting with medical professionals.

senses.definition: to make something end, particularly by ruining what was planned

examples.x: The unexpected rainstorm put paid to our beach vacation plans.
The economic downturn has put paid to the company's ambitious expansion strategy.
The new security measures are intended to put paid to any unauthorized access to the sensitive data.
After numerous delays, the government's infrastructure project finally put paid to the traffic congestion issue.

senses.definition: to prevent something from happening or making more progress

examples.x: Faced with rising costs, the company had to put the brakes on their ambitious expansion plans.
Concerns about environmental impact led the government to put the brakes on the construction of the new highway.
To avoid burnout, Jane realized she needed to put the brakes on her hectic work schedule.

senses.definition: to put a sudden stop to something one was doing for some time

examples.x: After years of working nonstop, I've decided to give my career a rest and focus on my personal life.
I've been dieting for months, but now I need to give it a rest and enjoy some indulgent meals.
She's been studying for hours on end, so her friends suggested she give her books a rest and take a break.
He's been trying to fix that old car for weeks; it might be time to give it a rest and call a mechanic.

senses.definition: to accept the fact that one has lost something and might never be able to get it back

examples.x: When she missed her flight, she had to kiss her vacation plans goodbye.

word_list.pronunciation: ɡˈɪv ˌʌp ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ æz ɐ bˈæd dʒˈɑːb

senses.definition: to decide that it is impossible to help something succeed because there seems to be no hope
to prepare oneself for death, dismissal, etc.

examples.x: I knew I'd kissed my ass goodbye after I talked back to the sergeant during training.
When the CEO found out about the data breach, we all knew the IT team could kiss their ass goodbye.
The politician knew his career was over and he'd have to kiss his ass goodbye after the scandal broke.

senses.definition: to put an end to something, particularly something bad, that has been going on for some time

examples.x: Detectives had closed the book on the Hornsey Murders case three years previously.
I think it's time for me to close the books on this situation because I'm clearly never getting my money back.
Investigators say DNA has helped close the books on two unsolved murders in California in the late 1980s .
It can be difficult to close the book on something you’ve worked on for so long.
It’s time to close the book on this unfortunate period in our history.
Rady made a motion to close the book on the matter.

senses.definition: something that does not exist or happen anymore due to being replaced by something more modern or desirable

examples.x: With the advent of digital cameras, film photography has become a thing of the past for most people.
Landline telephones have become a thing of the past, as the majority of individuals now use mobile phones.
The days of handwritten letters and paper correspondence are now a thing of the past with email and instant messaging.
Vinyl records were once the primary medium for music, but they are now considered a thing of the past with the rise of digital music streaming.
In the modern era, encyclopedias in printed form have become a thing of the past, as people turn to online sources for information.

word_list.pronunciation: wˈaɪp ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ ˈɔf ðə mˈæp fˈeɪs ʌvðɪ ˈɜːθ

senses.definition: to remove or destroy someone or something completely

examples.x: The natural disaster had the power to wipe entire towns off the map, leaving behind only debris.
The catastrophic explosion was so powerful that it could potentially wipe the entire neighborhood off the map.
The ruthless dictator threatened to use nuclear weapons to wipe entire nations off the face of the earth.

senses.definition: (of a quality, idea, or principle) to not longer exist or be forgotten

examples.x: When the unexpected financial crisis hit, the company's expansion plans went out the window.
His commitment to a healthy diet went out the window when he was presented with a tempting dessert.
In the excitement of the moment, their careful planning for the event went out the window.

senses.definition: (of a plan or course of action) to fail to succeed or produce the expected results

examples.x: The once-promising startup began to wither on the vine when it failed to secure additional funding.
The athlete's career started to wither on the vine after a series of injuries left him unable to compete.
The ambitious plans for the new product launch began to wither on the vine when the marketing team didn't follow through.
The community garden project began to wither on the vine when volunteers became too busy to maintain it.
Without proper support and resources, the artistic initiative started to wither on the vine, despite its creative potential.
